# Requirements from overview document

These assess the progress of the project, and provide an opportunity for preliminary feedback. Each student must contribute to the presentation of their project in order to: 

* Summarise the work done so far, flag issues and steps taken to overcome them   
* Where applicable, show an early prototype and demonstrate feasibility   
* Present a summary of team members' personal and technical development (what have you learnt and how have you learned it?)  
* Update the project development time-line and present a plan for completion 

Value will be given to: 

* team working skills  
* good records of work done and issues overcome  
* troubleshooting skills and the ability to reassess objectives  
* technical characterisation of how well the prototype meets the project objective

Each student should present for 5 minutes. Each student should ideally present a specific aspect they have contributed to (giving credit to others where appropriate) and briefly explain how the work package they present fits within the overall project. 

Marks are individual. They will reflect the overall progress of the project, the individual contribution to the project, and the quality of the presentation.

# Outline of Content

* Intro  
* Work done so far  
  * Change in scope of project  
    * reduced number of components to be made  
      * due to limited time  
    * focus on accessible and cheap materials with simple building techniques farmers should be able to build as even if there are experts and engineers, they are small in number and expensive to hire  
    * Focused on water bladder as it has not been used, but makes sense as a more direct conversion to heat for night-time, stops the middleman conversion to battery energy storage and electricity  
  * Research done  
    * Things/methods that have been tried  
      * [DIY aluminium can solar heater](https://www.instructables.com/Aluminum-Can-Solar-Heater/)  
      * Existing dryers ([section 2.3 of research paper](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022474X2300084X#sec2))  
  * Experiments ran  
* Issues and Steps Taken; Possible future work  
  * Fan battery does not last long enough  
    * fix: order portable chargers  
  * tedious and inconsistent to connect fan to bag  
    * maybe extend the fan outlet with a cylinder to make wrapping the bag around it easier  
* where applicable, Early prototype and demonstrate feasibility  
* Team members’ Personal and Technical Development  
* Updated Timeline  
* Plan for Completion
